name: Chicken Sauce Piquante
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the chicken generously with salt and pepper.
2. Melt 1/4 cup shortening in a large Dutch oven and sauté the chicken until brown on all sides.
3. Add water, celery, bell pepper, green onion, bay leaves, and mushrooms to the sauce; simmer.
4. In a saucepan, melt 3/4 cup shortening; add flour and cook slowly until dark brown, stirring constantly.
5. Add minced onions and garlic; sauté until translucent, stirring constantly.
6. Add tomato paste and cook until thickened.
7. Transfer this mixture to the Dutch oven and bring to a boil; reduce heat and simmer 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until chicken is tender and sauce is thick.
8. If the sauce thickens before the chicken is done, add water and stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
9. During the last 30 minutes of cooking, add salt, cayenne pepper (the more cayenne, the spicier the gravy will be), mushrooms, olives, lemon slices, and parsley.
10. Just before serving, add wine.
11. Serve over hot spaghetti or fluffy rice.
